label: Long Ring Long Land tidal ring island system

anchor.name: Long Ring Long Land
anchor.japanese: ロングリングロングランド

atlas_anchor.warning: The atlas point is deterministic route placement, not canon geography or a source for local bearings.

verified_relationships: Long Ring Long Land appears as ten island sections arranged in a ring.
The ten sections are parts of one island whose connecting land is normally submerged.
Once each year, low tide exposes the connecting path and permits travel between sections.
The island's vegetation, animals, and open land share a distinctive elongated visual identity.
interpolated_relationships: Each section outline, ring diameter, section spacing, submerged shelf, water depth, and land-bridge width are visual interpolation.
The local ring is composed for readability and carries no canon north, rotation, or measured circumference.
Elongated fauna proxies communicate scale only and must not be treated as exact encounter placement.
topology.transformation_policy: Normal runtime state shows one present island section. Chapter 305 unlocks an explanatory ring/base overlay, not an assertion that the annual low tide occurs during that chapter. No automatic chapter-driven tide transition is permitted.

chapter_logic.default_rule: Render the present section from chapter 304. Ring, submerged base, and annual low-tide connections remain explanatory overlays after chapter 305.
chapter_logic.unknown_gate_rule: Do not assign a chapter-state tide, exact section outline, or exact ring layout without direct panel-level evidence.

proposed_local_scene_layout.root: LRLL_ROOT at local origin with sea-level Z=0

zones.placement: camera-facing long grass plain with low stretched hills
nine additional low-detail sections around a normalized circle; exact spacing withheld

zones.purpose: show that the sections form one island
zones.placement: translucent or cutaway layer below sea level
stylized connecting shelf between sections; never used as a current-time claim

proposed_local_scene_layout.camera_read: Begin at ground level on one elongated plain; explanatory mode pulls up to a diagrammatic ring and underwater shared base.

unreal_runtime_reuse.unreal_notes: Instance one section archetype with authored variations for the diagrammatic ring. Use material fades for submerged/exposed explanatory layers; do not simulate a chapter-timed ocean tide.
unreal_runtime_reuse.map_runtime_notes: The normal map layer may load only the present section. The ten-section context should be a chapter-305 exposition overlay with explicit non-scale labeling.
unreal_runtime_reuse.lod_policy: Preserve the long horizontal silhouette and ring read; collapse small vegetation and fauna proxies first.

coordinate_policy.warning: No canon ring diameter, section bearings, tide height, or island dimensions are encoded.

integration_contract.node_gate_policy: Read component_id, reveal_chapter, classification, verification, and default_hidden from glTF extras.

unresolved: Exact outlines, dimensions, spacing, orientation, elevation, and ring diameter of the ten sections remain visual interpolation.
Exact depth and geometry of the common submerged island base are unknown.
The annual low-tide route is verified as a cycle but is not verified as the active physical state in chapter 305; it stays explanatory.
Exact flora/fauna species placement and elongated proportions are not geographic measurements and remain representative.
No relationship to other regional weather systems is encoded from secondary databook claims.
